reconstructed_from: review artifact and follow-up evaluation notes

sql_refs.aggregate_query: SELECT skill_name, total_uses, avg_quality, success_rate, last_used FROM skill_metrics WHERE skill_name='research';
sql_refs.raw_rows_query: SELECT timestamp, session_id, agent_role, skill_name, task_type, quality_score, outcome, notes FROM skill_performance WHERE skill_name='research' ORDER BY timestamp DESC;

available: aggregate and raw skill_performance evidence can be reconstructed live from state.db
missing: session_id linkage for all five raw rows
direct task-level provenance behind the five rows
explicit documented success-definition source at signal time
important_findings: All five rows have session_id = null.
All five rows have outcome = partial, not success.
The public signal's 0% success rate is consistent with the aggregate, but the deeper issue is that outcome semantics and quality semantics are not aligned enough for a hard regression reading.

smallest_next_move: identify where the success/outcome definition was encoded when these rows were written
check whether any neighboring artifacts can restore task-level provenance
only then choose a single minimal instrumentation intervention
